headline: OpenAI and Anthropic both filed confidentially for IPOs while Apple pushed a cheaper AI strategy at WWDC 2026.
body: OpenAI filed confidentially for an IPO about a week after Anthropic, escalating the public-market race between the two leading labs, even as Sam Altman's Tools for Humanity reportedly cut staff over weak revenue. Apple used WWDC 2026 to court smaller developers with cheaper AI and a new Core AI library that runs PyTorch models on Apple hardware, with Siri features built on vision LLMs and Gemini-derived models on Private Cloud Compute. On the technique side, distributed-training and inference infrastructure broke out, with throughput scaling, CPU offloading, and DeepSpeed Ulysses entering as new tags alongside an AI engineering push. Privacy-preserving methods including encrypted reasoning and Differential Privacy posted the strongest rotation ratios, and a New York Times report noted AI scooping researchers on a long-standing math problem.

cited_items.title: As OpenAI files for IPO, Sam Altman’s eye-scanning company is doing layoffs, report says
